title: Noticastrum diffusum
intro: Noticastrum diffusum, commonly known as "False Boldo," is a shrub-like plant native to South America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The leaves of Noticastrum diffusum have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Noticastrum diffusum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Noticastrum diffusum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, which is a family of trees and shrubs commonly known as the cashew family. The genus Noticastrum is relatively small and consists of several species, all of which are found in the Americas.
